sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Geo Group Upsizes Revolving Credit Facility for Flexibility
feed.summary: Geo Group (GEO) has increased its revolving credit facility by $100 million, bringing the total to $550 million. This move aims to boost financial flexibility, support future growth plans, and signals stronger backing from its banking partners. While a recent analyst rating suggests a "Buy" with a $35.00 price target, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, Spark, rates GEO as "Neutral" due to strong strategic progress balanced against liquidity concerns and mixed technical indicators.

feed.title: Johnson Controls appoints Susan Hughes to lead Asia Pacific region
feed.summary: Johnson Controls has appointed Susan Hughes as vice president and president, Asia Pacific, where she will report to CEO Joakim Weidemanis and join the executive committee. Hughes brings decades of operational experience from her 20-year career at Emerson, where she notably served as President of Asia Pacific for Emerson Automation Solutions. Her appointment is expected to strengthen execution and accelerate growth opportunities in the Asia Pacific region for Johnson Controls.

feed.title: CF Industries, POET, and Major Agriculture Co-Operatives Launch Low-Carbon Fertilizer Pilot to Cut Ethanol Production Carbon Intensity
feed.summary: CF Industries, POET, and major agriculture co-operatives have launched a pilot project to develop a low-carbon fertilizer supply chain. The initiative aims to reduce the carbon intensity of corn by using low-carbon nitrogen fertilizer, thereby enabling the production of low-carbon ethanol. This collaboration includes WinField United and agricultural cooperatives NuWay-K&H, New Cooperative, and Farmer’s Cooperative, with POET expecting to produce 5-6 million gallons of lower carbon intensity ethanol.

feed.title: FAT Brands Initiates Voluntary Chapter 11 Proceedings
feed.summary: FAT Brands has voluntarily filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of Texas to deleverage its balance sheet and maximize stakeholder value. Despite the filing, the company's 18 restaurant concepts, including popular brands like Fatburger and Johnny Rockets, will continue to operate normally. FAT Brands is also engaging restructuring advisors to help with its debt restructuring plan and aims to raise $75 million to $100 million in equity to fund the opening of 80 new locations.

feed.title: Waste Management (WM) to report earnings tomorrow: Here is what to expect
feed.summary: This article announces that Waste Management (WM) is scheduled to report its earnings tomorrow. Investors and analysts are anticipating the report to gain insights into the company's financial performance and future outlook. The article likely aims to set expectations or provide a brief preview of what to look for in the upcoming earnings announcement.

feed.title: Lightmatter Collaborates with Synopsys to Integrate Advanced Interface IP with Its Passage Co-Packaged Optics Platform
feed.summary: Lightmatter is partnering with Synopsys to integrate Synopsys' 224G SerDes and UCIe IP for 3nm process into Lightmatter's Passage 3D Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) platform. This collaboration aims to create a robust, low-latency CPO platform for next-generation AI infrastructure, optimizing the electrical-to-optical interface and ensuring high-bandwidth connectivity for AI accelerators. The partnership seeks to maximize performance, reliability, and power efficiency for hyperscale AI by enhancing bandwidth, reducing design risk, and accelerating time-to-market.

feed.title: Ex-Citi Exec Says Rampant Misogyny Was A 'Price Too Steep'
feed.summary: A former high-ranking director at Citigroup has filed a lawsuit in New York federal court, alleging that rampant misogyny and false workplace rumors forced her out of her job. She claims to have been "debased and humiliated" by suggestions that she engaged in sexual relations to secure a promotion. The lawsuit highlights a persistent misogynistic culture at the investment bank.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines Deploys Retro Boeing 737 MAX 8 To Operate Final Flight With Open Seating
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines is ending its long-standing open seating policy, with the final flight operating with open seating set to depart Honolulu on January 26, 2026. This flight will be operated by a Boeing 737 MAX 8 in the original "Desert Gold" livery, a tribute to co-founder Herb Kelleher. The change marks a shift for Southwest towards industry norms, allowing for monetization through assigned seating and extra-legroom options, following significant shareholder investment.

feed.title: Viant Technology Q2 2024 Financial Results
feed.summary: Viant Technology announced strong Q2 2024 financial results, with significant revenue and gross profit increases, and a return to net income. The company highlighted robust growth in CTV and streaming audio, alongside strategic AI initiatives and continued share repurchases. Viant also provided an optimistic outlook for Q3 2024, projecting further revenue and Adjusted EBITDA growth.

feed.title: Karooooo Ltd. (KARO) Recently Broke Out Above the 200-Day Moving Average
feed.summary: Karooooo Ltd. (KARO) recently broke above its 200-day moving average, signaling a potential long-term bullish trend and making it an attractive pick for investors. The company has seen a 5.2% rally over the past four weeks and holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy). Positive earnings estimate revisions further strengthen the bullish outlook for KARO.

feed.title: Starbucks (SBUX) Reports Earnings Tomorrow: What To Expect
feed.summary: Starbucks (SBUX) is set to report its Q4 earnings before market hours on Wednesday. Analysts expect revenue to grow 2.8% year-on-year to $9.66 billion and adjusted earnings of $0.59 per share, despite the company missing Wall Street's revenue estimates five times in the last two years. The restaurant segment has shown positive investor sentiment recently, with Starbucks' stock up 12.4% over the last month.

feed.title: China's BYD, Exxon Mobil to deepen hybrid technology partnership
feed.summary: China's BYD and U.S. oil major Exxon Mobil are set to broaden their collaboration in hybrid technology based on a newly signed long-term strategic Memorandum of Understanding. This agreement will focus on exploring customized product research and development, and new material applications. The partnership comes after the companies jointly launched engine oil specifically designed for BYD's plug-in electric vehicles last year.

feed.title: Enphase Energy (ENPH) upgraded to 'neutral', price target raised by $6
feed.summary: BMO Capital Markets has upgraded Enphase Energy (ENPH) to 'neutral' from 'underperform' and increased its price target by $6, now set at $117. The upgrade reflects a more balanced risk/reward outlook for the company, aligning with current market sentiment.

feed.title: ADP (ADP) Reports Earnings Tomorrow: What To Expect
feed.summary: Automatic Data Processing (ADP) is set to release its earnings this Wednesday. Analysts anticipate a 5.5% year-on-year revenue growth to $5.33 billion and adjusted earnings of $2.57 per share. The company has a history of consistently beating revenue estimates and analysts have reconfirmed their estimates ahead of the announcement.

feed.title: Expedia Cuts Jobs in Fresh Round of Layoffs
feed.summary: Expedia Group is conducting another round of layoffs as part of a broader effort to streamline its organization and prepare for future skill requirements. While the company has not specified how many employees are affected or which departments are targeted, it is simultaneously advertising over 250 open positions. These changes follow previous layoffs in 2024 and 2025 and align with Expedia's recent investments in AI leadership and strategy.

feed.title: King Sarah H, SVP at Darden Restaurants, sells $951k in stock
feed.summary: Sarah H. King, SVP and Chief People Officer at Darden Restaurants Inc. (NYSE:DRI), sold 4,605 shares of common stock for a total of $951,636 across two transactions on January 22, 2026. Following these sales, King directly owns 670.7710 shares. This insider trading comes as Darden maintains a "GOOD" financial health score and steady dividend payments, with InvestingPro data noting the stock trades near its Fair Value.

feed.title: Does EFSC’s Strong Q3 Loan and Deposit Growth Change The Bull Case For Enterprise Financial Services?
feed.summary: Enterprise Financial Services (EFSC) reported solid Q3 results with annualized loan growth of 6% and even higher deposit growth, boosted by a 12-branch acquisition. While analysts' expectations for revenue and EPS remained stable, the market's modest reaction suggests the news is important but not thesis-changing, especially considering ongoing credit quality concerns related to nonperforming assets and commercial real estate. Investors should weigh the reinforced growth narrative against potential risks from credit strains.

feed.title: Halliburton’s NEX Lab Partnership And What It May Mean For Valuation
feed.summary: Halliburton has launched the Next-Generation Energy Accelerators Joint Lab (NEX Lab) in Singapore, partnering with A*STAR and the Economic Development Board to develop advanced energy and low-carbon technologies. This initiative strengthens Halliburton's R&D in Asia and aims to integrate new low-carbon applications with its core well completion business. While the stock is currently flagged as undervalued by Simply Wall St, investors should monitor how NEX Lab's work translates into commercial offerings and impacts Halliburton's profitability in the future.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Ramaco Resources (METC) After Its 138.6% One Year Surge?
feed.summary: Ramaco Resources (METC) has seen a significant 138.6% surge over the past year, leading to questions about its current valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is overvalued by 153.1% at its current price of $22.09, with a fair value estimated at $8.73 per share. Similarly, its Price-to-Sales (P/S) ratio of 2.52x is above its calculated Fair Ratio of 1.00x, also indicating overvaluation.

feed.title: Goldman’s Solomon Sees ‘Slower’ Trajectory for Talent Growth
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon anticipates a "slower" trajectory for talent growth in 2026, driven by a focus on efficiency and strategic investment in scaling growth areas. Despite this, he expressed optimism for global markets due to a de-regulatory environment. Solomon noted that efficiency opportunities allow the firm to invest in business segments that can grow, sometimes requiring additional headcount.

feed.title: Gauzy Ltd. Announces Changes to Management and Board of Directors
feed.summary: Gauzy Ltd. announced changes to its management and Board of Directors, including the resignation of CFO Meir Peleg and the appointment of Dan Oshri as Interim CFO. Board members Lilach Payorski and Alexander Babitsky also stepped down. The company also disclosed a proposal for a $50 million equity line of credit facility from an existing shareholder to address liquidity challenges and support operations.

feed.title: Beyond The Numbers: 4 Analysts Discuss GRAIL Stock
feed.summary: Four analysts have provided ratings for GRAIL (NASDAQ: GRAL) in the last quarter, with an average price target of $111.25, reflecting a 23.61% increase from the prior average. The company, focused on early cancer detection, shows positive revenue growth but lags behind peers in its sector, while demonstrating strong net margins and conservative debt management. Analysts' assessments are crucial for investors seeking insights into GRAIL's market standing and future prospects.

feed.title: Hycroft Mining (HYMC) Is Up 43.2% After Shelf Filing And Record Vortex Silver Grades - Has The Bull Case Changed?
feed.summary: Hycroft Mining (HYMC) has seen a significant stock increase after filing a US$163.24 million shelf registration and reporting record silver grades at its Vortex Silver System. This development highlights the company's dual strategy of securing funding through equity and advancing exploration, particularly amidst high silver prices. Despite the positive news, the article suggests that while exploration success strengthens the company's narrative, dilution risk and execution challenges remain key considerations for investors.

feed.title: Form DEF 14A Keysight Technologies, For: Mar 19
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies (KEYS) has filed its definitive proxy statement (Form DEF 14A) for its annual stockholder meeting scheduled for March 19, 2026. The meeting will be held virtually and will cover the election of three directors, ratification of PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm, an advisory vote on executive compensation, and a proposal to declassify the Board of Directors. The board also addresses a stockholder proposal for shareholders to call special meetings, recommending a vote against it due to potential inefficiencies and strong existing corporate governance practices.

feed.title: Enterprise Financial reports $54.8M in Q4 net income as deposits surge by $1B
feed.summary: Enterprise Financial reported a Q4 net income of $54.8 million. The St. Louis-based bank experienced a significant increase in deposits, which surged by approximately $1 billion during the quarter. This growth was partly attributed to strategic branch acquisitions that also brought in hundreds of millions in new loans.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says Kimberly-Clark’s Merger With Kenvue “Could Keep This Stock in Its Place”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer is "warming up" to Kimberly-Clark (NASDAQ: KMB) and anticipates a strong quarter for the company. However, he suggests that a potential merger with Kenvue, involving a cash and stock deal, could limit the stock's upward movement due to Kenvue's inherent issues. Despite this, Cramer notes Kimberly-Clark's 5% yield and 13 times earnings make it appear intriguing, especially after observing the performance of competitors like Procter & Gamble and Clorox.

feed.title: Daxor Signs Multiple Material Agreements
feed.summary: Daxor (DXR) has entered into two significant agreements to support a registered direct equity offering. The company signed a Securities Purchase Agreement with investors to sell 765,958 common shares at $11.75 per share, resulting in approximately $8.14 million in net proceeds. Additionally, Daxor signed a Placement Agency Agreement with Lake Street Capital Markets, which facilitated the transaction that closed on January 26, 2026, with proceeds earmarked for general corporate and growth needs.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) Q4 Earnings: What To Expect
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson (NASDAQ:CHRW) is scheduled to report its Q4 earnings this Wednesday. Analysts expect the company's revenue to decline 4.7% year-on-year to $3.99 billion and adjusted earnings to be $1.13 per share. The company has missed Wall Street's revenue estimates multiple times in the past two years, but investor sentiment in the transportation and logistics sector has been positive recently.

feed.title: Fair Isaac Corporation (FICO) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
feed.summary: Fair Isaac Corporation (FICO) is set to report its earnings tomorrow after market hours. Analysts anticipate a 14.3% year-on-year revenue growth to $502.7 million and adjusted earnings of $7.08 per share. Despite mixed past performance and recent stock decline, analysts have reconfirmed their estimates, suggesting stability heading into the earnings report.

feed.title: Lam Research Earnings: What To Look For From LRCX
feed.summary: Lam Research is set to report its latest earnings this Wednesday after market close, with analysts expecting revenue growth of 20% year over year to $5.25 billion and adjusted earnings of $1.17 per share. The company has a history of consistently beating revenue estimates. Investor sentiment in the semiconductor segment has been positive, with Lam Research's stock outperforming peers recently.

feed.title: King Sarah H, SVP at Darden Restaurants, sells $951k in stock
feed.summary: Sarah H. King, SVP, Chief People Officer at Darden Restaurants Inc, sold 4,605 shares of common stock on January 22, 2026, totaling $951,636. Following these transactions, King directly owns 670.7710 shares. Darden Restaurants has maintained dividend payments for 32 consecutive years and analysts have recently provided varied but generally positive outlooks on the company.

feed.title: IBM (IBM) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
feed.summary: IBM is scheduled to report earnings tomorrow, with expectations for 9.5% year-on-year revenue growth to $19.21 billion and adjusted earnings of $4.29 per share. Analysts have largely maintained their estimates, suggesting a consistent performance is anticipated. Despite some past revenue misses, peer performance in the IT services sector and IBM's current financial position (with an average analyst price target above its current share price) provide context for the upcoming report.

feed.title: Waste Management (WM) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
feed.summary: Waste Management (WM) is set to report its earnings tomorrow after market close, with analysts expecting revenue growth of 8.5% year-on-year to $6.39 billion and adjusted earnings of $1.95 per share. The company missed revenue expectations last quarter and has done so four times in the last two years. Despite this, positive investor sentiment in the environmental and facilities services segment has seen share prices rise, with WM's stock up 3.5% over the last month, approaching an average analyst price target of $248.56.

feed.title: Carnival Corporation named one of the World’s most admired companies for second consecutive year
feed.summary: Carnival Corporation has been recognized for the second consecutive year on Fortune’s World’s Most Admired Companies list. The world's largest cruise company was included based on its strong industry leadership, financial performance, and global competitiveness, making it one of only eight companies in the Hotels, Casinos and Resorts category. CEO Josh Weinstein credited the company's 160,000 global team members for this achievement.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) Q4 Earnings: What To Expect
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) is set to report its Q4 earnings on Wednesday. Analysts expect revenue to decrease by 4.7% year-on-year to $3.99 billion and adjusted earnings per share to be $1.13. The company has missed Wall Street’s revenue estimates multiple times recently, while the transportation and logistics segment has shown positive investor sentiment.

feed.title: United Rentals (URI) To Report Earnings Tomorrow: Here Is What To Expect
feed.summary: United Rentals (URI) is set to report its earnings this Wednesday. The company beat revenue expectations last quarter but missed EPS estimates. Analysts predict a 3.4% year-on-year revenue growth to $4.24 billion and adjusted earnings of $11.80 per share.

feed.title: Earnings To Watch: AT&T (T) Reports Q4 Results Tomorrow
feed.summary: AT&T (NYSE: T) is set to announce its Q4 earnings tomorrow, with analysts expecting revenues to grow 1.5% year-on-year to $32.79 billion and adjusted earnings of $0.47 per share. The company met revenue expectations last quarter but missed Mobility revenue estimates, and its stock is down 4.9% over the last month. Investors will be watching keenly after mixed results from peers in the consumer discretionary segment.

feed.title: Textron Earnings: What To Look For From TXT
feed.summary: Textron (TXT) is scheduled to report earnings this Wednesday before market open, with analysts expecting revenue growth of 12.9% year-on-year to $4.08 billion and adjusted earnings of $1.70 per share. The company has a history of missing Wall Street’s revenue estimates and investors are watching closely, especially given recent mixed performance from peers in the aerospace and defense sector. Textron's stock is up 7.4% recently and analysts have an average price target of $98.41.

feed.title: Earnings To Watch: Corning (GLW) Reports Q4 Results Tomorrow
feed.summary: Corning (GLW) is set to report its Q4 earnings tomorrow, with analysts expecting a 12.8% year-on-year revenue growth to $4.37 billion and adjusted earnings of $0.71 per share. The company has a strong record of exceeding revenue estimates, beating them every quarter over the last two years. Positive sentiment in the electrical equipment sector, where Corning operates, suggests a favorable environment as the company approaches its earnings release.

feed.title: Harlem’s Dollar Tree fined $560K for selling lead-tainted baby food after recall
feed.summary: New York Attorney General Letitia James announced that Dollar Tree will pay a $559,250 penalty for selling lead-contaminated WanaBana cinnamon applesauce pouches even after a nationwide recall in October 2023. The investigation revealed that Dollar Tree delayed activating sales prevention measures, allowing hundreds of units to be sold for days and even weeks post-recall. The settlement funds will support lead poisoning prevention programs and food access in New York, and Dollar Tree must also overhaul its recall procedures.

feed.title: What To Expect From Danaher’s (DHR) Q4 Earnings
feed.summary: Danaher (DHR) is set to report its Q4 earnings on Wednesday, with analysts expecting revenue growth of 4.2% year-on-year to $6.81 billion and adjusted earnings of $2.19 per share. The company has a history of exceeding revenue estimates and is heading into earnings with positive investor sentiment in the life sciences tools & services sector. Danaher's stock is up 2.8% in the last month, with an average analyst price target of $263.05.

feed.title: MSCI (MSCI) Q4 Earnings Report Preview: What To Look For
feed.summary: MSCI (NYSE:MSCI), an investment analytics provider, is set to report its Q4 earnings before market open this Wednesday. Analysts anticipate an 11% year-on-year revenue growth to $824.9 million and adjusted earnings of $4.58 per share. While MSCI met revenue expectations last quarter, it has missed Wall Street's revenue estimates three times in the past two years.

feed.title: Ford and General Motors in talks with First Brands over rescue financing, FT reports
feed.summary: Ford and General Motors are in negotiations with bankrupt car parts supplier First Brands Group over a potential financing package. The financing aims to keep First Brands operating during its Chapter 11 proceedings, as reported by the Financial Times. First Brands has begun winding down parts of its North American operations while seeking buyers for remaining assets.

feed.title: The Problem Lockheed Martin Ran Into When Delivering The F-35 Last Year
feed.summary: In 2025, Lockheed Martin delivered a record 191 F-35 Joint Strike Fighters, a figure that on the surface suggested a positive turning point for the program. However, this surge in deliveries was primarily due to the clearing of a backlog caused by delays in certifying the Technology Refresh 3 (TR-3) upgrade. The TR-3 upgrade, vital for the F-35's modernization and future capabilities, experienced significant software integration and testing challenges, leading to a pause in deliveries throughout much of 2023 and 2024, despite continued production.

feed.title: Salling Group joins WPP Media's Open Intelligence for Commerce 01/27/2026
feed.summary: Salling Group, Denmark's largest retailer, has partnered with WPP Media to integrate its first-party data with WPP’s Open Intelligence for Commerce platform. This collaboration will connect Salling's weekly customer data from its retail chains across six European countries with WPP's platform to generate commerce insights and enable custom targeting for WPP clients. Salling's own retail media network is also expected to benefit from this partnership, enhancing data-driven insights and media activation while maintaining privacy standards.

feed.title: Ellington Financial launches 8.7 million share offering to redeem preferred stock
feed.summary: Ellington Financial Inc. has announced a public offering of 8.775 million shares of common stock, with an option for underwriters to purchase additional shares. The company plans to use the net proceeds to redeem its Series A Preferred Stock and for general corporate purposes, including potential asset acquisitions. This follows a strong Q3 2025 earnings performance, exceeding analyst forecasts, and an expansion of its at-the-market stock offering.

feed.title: Nova Minerals secures US$43.4m DoW funding as antimony strategy advances in December quarter
feed.summary: Nova Minerals received US$43.4 million (A$65.5 million) in non-dilutive funding from the US Department of War (DoW), fully financing the first stage of developing a domestic antimony supply chain at its Estelle Project in Alaska. This funding significantly strengthens Nova's balance sheet, supporting its goal to become a fully integrated US-based producer of military-grade antimony, with production anticipated to commence in late 2026 to early 2027. The company also progressed its gold assets at Estelle, completing a drilling program that confirmed mineralisation and continuing pre-feasibility study work.

feed.title: Jack In The Box Rolls Out Matcha For Its 75th Anniversary
feed.summary: Jack in the Box is introducing a dedicated matcha platform as part of its 2026 lineup, coinciding with its 75th anniversary. This move signifies the brand's confidence in matcha as a mainstream, long-term category rather than a fleeting trend. The launch is paired with a limited Valentine's Day promotion featuring heart-shaped straws.

feed.title: Micron Breaks Ground on Advanced Wafer Fabrication Facility in Singapore
feed.summary: Micron Technology has broken ground on an advanced wafer fabrication facility in Singapore, representing a planned investment of approximately US $24 billion over 10 years. The facility, which will ultimately provide 700,000 square feet of cleanroom space and begin wafer output in late 2028, aims to meet growing market demand for NAND technology driven by AI. This expansion is expected to create around 1,600 new jobs, complementing another 1,400 jobs from an HBM advanced packaging facility, further solidifying Singapore's role in the global semiconductor supply chain.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Starbucks: "It Can't Keep Climbing Unless the Numbers Are Insanely Strong"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented on Starbucks (NASDAQ: SBUX), deeming the stock "wildly overbought" ahead of its earnings report and an investor day. He believes that for the stock to continue climbing, its numbers need to be exceptionally strong, as its recent rally may not be justifiable otherwise. Cramer likes Starbucks for the long term but doubts the company can tell a story sufficient to support its current valuation.
